title: Quiz: Classical-Quantum System Architecture Design Patterns

questions.question: In a layered hybrid quantum-classical architecture, which layer is responsible for backend-agnostic circuit representation and transpilation management?
questions.explanation: The Quantum Abstraction Layer is responsible for backend-agnostic circuit representation, transpilation management, error mitigation configuration, and result post-processing. It sits between the Orchestration Layer and the Quantum Execution Layer.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the Facade pattern in hybrid quantum systems?

options.text: To allow runtime selection of different optimization algorithms
To provide a simplified interface to complex quantum subsystems
To enable communication between different quantum backends

questions.explanation: The Facade pattern provides a simplified interface to complex quantum subsystems. It hides the complexity of circuit creation, transpilation, execution, and result processing behind a simple API like run_vqe() or run_qaoa().

questions.question: In the variational algorithm workflow pattern, what is the role of the cost function?

options.text: To initialize the quantum circuit parameters
To bridge classical optimizer and quantum circuit execution
To select the appropriate quantum backend
To handle errors in the quantum execution

questions.explanation: The cost function serves as the core interface between the classical optimizer and quantum circuit execution. It takes parameters from the classical optimizer, executes the quantum circuit, calculates the expectation value, and returns it to the optimizer.

questions.question: Which design pattern allows you to switch between different quantum backends (IBM, Amazon Braket, IonQ) using a uniform interface?
questions.explanation: The Adapter pattern provides a uniform interface to different quantum backends. It allows the same code to work with IBM, Amazon Braket, IonQ, or any other backend by implementing a common interface (submit_circuit, get_result, get_backend_properties).

questions.question: When should tight coupling be preferred over loose coupling in hybrid quantum-classical systems?

options.text: When you need backend flexibility and easy testing
When building performance-critical optimization loops and real-time feedback systems
When connecting application layer to quantum abstraction layer

questions.explanation: Tight coupling is preferred within performance-critical optimization loops, real-time feedback systems, and hardware-specific optimizations where low latency is crucial. Loose coupling is better for modularity and flexibility between layers.

questions.question: What does the Strategy pattern enable in the context of variational quantum algorithms?

options.text: Dynamic selection of quantum hardware at runtime
Runtime selection of classical optimization algorithms

questions.explanation: The Strategy pattern allows runtime selection of optimization algorithms (COBYLA, SPSA, Adam, etc.) for variational quantum circuits. The OptimizerContext class can switch between different OptimizationStrategy implementations without changing the client code.

questions.question: In a layered hybrid architecture, lower layers (like Quantum Execution) should depend on higher layers (like Application) to maintain proper separation of concerns.
questions.explanation: False. In a properly designed layered architecture, dependencies should flow downward - higher layers depend on lower layers, not the reverse. The Quantum Execution Layer should have no dependencies on upper layers, while the Application Layer depends on the Orchestration Layer.

questions.question: Which of the following are responsibilities of the Orchestration Layer in a hybrid quantum-classical architecture? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The Orchestration Layer is responsible for classical-quantum coordination, parameter optimization loops, error handling and retries, and resource scheduling. Direct backend communication belongs to the Quantum Execution Layer, and user interface components belong to the Presentation Layer.
